# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 100

This is turn 100. Turns 1 through 99 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 100.

## Turn 100 — one-input closure of the fixed-frame proof

The submission remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation \(\tau^\sigma=\tau^2\) is implicit. In the mathematical presentation, the exponent is the genuine idempotent \(\omega _2\in\widehat{\mathbf Z}\); \(40491355905\) is its required verifier residue modulo \(85667662080\). 

Turn 98 gave a fixed-tame-frame proof conditional on Thompson criticality and a dyadic Kummer/Hilbert theorem. Turn 99 replaced the Thompson input by a direct Frattini–centralizer argument. 

### The sole remaining imported theorem

The complete local-field input can be compressed to the following exact numerical statement.

**Dyadic determinant theorem.** Let \(V\) be a nontrivial self-dual simple tame \(\mathbf F _2\)-module for \(G_{\mathbf Q _2}\), of dimension \(d\), and let \(q\) be an invariant nonsingular quadratic form on \(V\). Let
\[
Q_{\mathrm{loc}}\colon H^1(\mathbf Q _2,V)\longrightarrow
H^2(\mathbf Q _2,\mathbf F _2)\simeq\mathbf F _2
\]
be the connecting quadratic form of the associated central extension. Then
\[
\#Q_{\mathrm{loc}}^{-1}(0)=
\begin{cases}
2^{d-1}-2^{d/2-1},&V\text{ unramified},\\[2mm]
2^{d-1}+2^{d/2-1},&V\text{ ramified}.
\end{cases}
\tag{100.1}
\]

Equivalently, the unramified form is minus type and the ramified form is hyperbolic. This follows from the standard dyadic Kummer filtration, the Hilbert orthogonality
\[
U_i^\perp=U_{2e-i+1},
\]
and \(a\smile a=a\smile(-1)\).

The candidate-side form is already internal to the word calculation. If \(S,T\) are the tame actions and \(U=S^{\omega _2}\), then
\[
Q_A(c)=
\begin{cases}
q(c),&T=1,\\
q(c)+B(c,U^{-1}c),&V^T=0.
\end{cases}
\tag{100.2}
\]
The Wall-form calculation in the preceding sweep gives the same two zero counts as (100.1). Thus
\[
\#Q_A^{-1}(i)=\#Q_{\mathrm{loc}}^{-1}(i),
\qquad i=0,1.
\tag{100.3}
\]

No finite-group classification theorem remains.

### Direct collapse of the minimal active block

Fix a tame frame and let \(L\triangleleft G\) be the wild \(2\)-kernel. Choose
\[
S\triangleleft P\triangleleft G,\qquad P/S\simeq V,
\]
where all chief factors below \(S\) are scalar and \(V\) is the first nontrivial simple factor. Let
\[
K\triangleleft G,\qquad K\le P,\qquad KS=P
\]
be minimal, and put
\[
R=\Phi(K).
\]

Let \(N\) be the odd kernel of the maximal \(2\)-quotient of \(G/L\), and let \(\widetilde N\) be an odd Hall lift.

Because every chief factor below \(S\) is trivial, coprime action along a chief series gives
\[
[S,\widetilde N]=1.
\tag{100.4}
\]
Since \(K/(K\cap S)\simeq V\) is elementary abelian,
\[
R\le K\cap S,
\]
so \(\widetilde N\) centralizes \(R\).

Set \(C=C_K(R)\). The subgroup \(C\) is characteristic in \(K\), hence normal in \(G\). From
\[
[R,\widetilde N]=1,\qquad [R,K]\le R,
\]
the three-subgroup lemma gives
\[
[K,\widetilde N]\le C.
\tag{100.5}
\]

The action of \(N\) on \(V\) is nontrivial: otherwise it would factor through a \(2\)-group, whose only simple module in characteristic \(2\) is trivial. Since \(V\) is simple,
\[
[V,N]=V.
\]
Consequently
\[
[K,\widetilde N]S=P.
\]
Thus \(CS=P\), and the minimality of \(K\) forces \(C=K\). Hence
\[
R\le Z(K).
\tag{100.6}
\]

Now \(K/R\) is elementary abelian, so \(x^2\in R\le Z(K)\) for every \(x\in K\). Therefore
\[
[x,y]^2=[x^2,y]=1.
\]
The fourth-power map
\[
f\colon K\longrightarrow R,\qquad f(x)=x^4
\]
is consequently a homomorphism: the class-two collection formula has only the factor \([y,x]^6=1\).

It is \(G\)-equivariant. Since \(\widetilde N\) fixes \(R\),
\[
f([k,n])=f(k)^{-1}f(k^n)=1.
\]
Let
\[
J=\langle [K,\widetilde N]^G\rangle.
\]
Then \(JS=P\), so minimality again gives \(J=K\). Equivariance shows that \(f\) vanishes on every conjugate of \([K,\widetilde N]\), hence on all of \(K\). Therefore
\[
K^4=1.
\tag{100.7}
\]

It follows that
\[
R=\Phi(K)=K^2[K,K]
\]
is central elementary abelian. Notice that the proof never assumes \([K,\widetilde N]\) itself is normal; only its \(G\)-normal closure is used. This directly supplies the sole conclusion formerly obtained from Thompson criticality. 

### One-input fixed-frame theorem

Let
\[
\alpha:T_{\mathrm{tame}}\twoheadrightarrow H=G/L
\]
be a fixed complete tame frame, together with an elementary scalar decoration. For either source
\[
\Gamma\in\{\Gamma_A,G_{\mathbf Q _2}\},
\]
write \(E_\Gamma(J)\) for the decorated fixed-frame maps whose exact image is a specified subgroup \(J\le G\) projecting onto \(H\).

Then, assuming only the dyadic determinant theorem,
\[
|E_{\Gamma_A}(J)|=|E_{G_{\mathbf Q _2}}(J)|
\tag{100.8}
\]
for every such \(J\).

The proof is strong induction on the order of the marked wild kernel.

If every wild chief factor is scalar, the target is the canonical pullback of the fixed tame quotient and a finite \(2\)-group. Its complete exact-image theory is determined by the common marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient
\[
\Pi=
\left\langle
\sigma,x_0,x_1\ \middle|\ 
x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}.
\tag{100.9}
\]

Otherwise choose the first nontrivial factor and the minimal \(K\) above. The direct collapse lemma gives
\[
R=\Phi(K)\ \text{central elementary},\qquad M=K/R\ \text{elementary}.
\]

With
\[
T_0=(K\cap S)/R,
\]
minimality gives
\[
T_0=\operatorname{rad}(M),\qquad
M/T_0\simeq V,\qquad
(M^\vee)^{G/K}=0.
\tag{100.10}
\]

For the \(M\)-lift, the common finite-Heisenberg deformation complex gives perfect duality
\[
H_\rho^i(M)\times H_\rho^{2-i}(M^\vee)\longrightarrow\mathbf F _2
\]
and hence
\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(M)^\vee\simeq(M^\vee)^{G/K}=0,
\qquad
\dim Z_\rho^1(M)=2\dim M.
\tag{100.11}
\]
Thus the total \(M\)-lift multiplicity agrees. Every proper exact image has a strictly smaller marked wild kernel and is covered by induction.

It remains to lift through \(R\). Put
\[
D=(R^\vee)^{G/R}.
\]
Finite-Heisenberg duality identifies \(D\) with the full character group of the \(R\)-valued relation obstruction. Fourier inversion reduces the zero-obstruction count to scalar pushouts indexed by \(\lambda\in D\).

For \(\lambda\ne0\), the simple-head argument gives precisely two possibilities:

1. a nonzero filtration-one edge, producing a nonzero affine functional and therefore an exact half-zero fibre;

2. a determinant component on the simple head \(V\).

For determinant components, nonsplit layers are killed by the \(d_2\)-transgression. In split layers, the marking-preserving shear removes every mixed edge term. The remaining unrestricted quadratic fibre is governed by (100.3).

Proper weighted image strata and inflated base constants are reduced uniformly to ordinary exact-image counts by the central-cover identity
\[
8u_\Gamma(p,J)=
\sum_{\substack{\widetilde J\le\widetilde Y\\p(\widetilde J)=J}}
|E_\Gamma(\widetilde J)|
\tag{100.12}
\]
for a marked central \(C_2\)-cover \(p:\widetilde Y\twoheadrightarrow Y\). Every target on the right has strictly smaller marked wild-kernel order.

Consequently every Fourier coefficient of the \(R\)-obstruction agrees. Once the obstruction vanishes, the two sources have the same \(R\)-lift multiplicity. Every such lift is surjective: if its image maps onto \(G/R\), then
\[
(J\cap K)R=K,
\]
and \(R=\Phi(K)\) forces \(J\cap K=K\).

This proves (100.8).

### Consequence

Taking \(J=G\), summing over all tame frames, removing decorations, and setting
\[
L=O_2(G)
\]
gives
\[
|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_A,G)|
=
|\operatorname{Sur}(G_{\mathbf Q _2},G)|
\tag{100.13}
\]
for every finite group \(G\), conditional only on the standard dyadic determinant theorem.

Thus the entire finite-group side of the proof is now internal: Thompson criticality and its bibliographic gap have disappeared. The unchanged candidate would therefore give the desired profinite presentation once the local Kummer/Hilbert proposition is accepted. The private 5402-group verifier has still not been executed. 

**ESTABLISHED**: Replaced Input T in the Turn-98 theorem by the direct Frattini–centralizer collapse lemma. The resulting fixed-frame exact-image theorem now has only one imported mathematical input: the standard dyadic Kummer/Hilbert determinant zero-count theorem. No external finite-group theorem remains.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier is unrun. The sole remaining proof dependency is the local dyadic determinant theorem (100.1), which should be written as a fully cited standalone local-field proposition.

**NEXT**: Eliminate the last external package by deriving (100.1) directly from the dyadic square-class filtration and Hilbert-symbol orthogonality, with all filtration indices and the unramified norm–trace case written explicitly.
